description: Light enters through 'mirae', the organ that enables sight. The noun identifies the body part; 'nila' (see) states the perceptual event, while attention and understanding keep their own vocabulary.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced 'm', carry the sound through the nose, and release into high-front unrounded 'i'. Make a tap or short trill at the alveolar ridge, then open into stressed central unrounded 'a'. Move the tongue forward into middle-height unrounded 'e' for the separate final syllable.

semantic_domains.cognition: the sensory organ through which visual perception reaches awareness
